params.name: Avesta
params.description:
On Apr 14, 395 BC, the religious texts of Zoroastrianism, Avesta, were compiled into a book.
Avesta is the most important collection of religious texts of Zoroastrianism. It mainly describes the dualism of goodness and evil, showing the conflict between the gods of the two sides and the conclusion that goodness will eventually win over evil. According to its theory, believers should adhere to the three principles of good thoughts, good words, and good deeds, and keep worshiping fire that symb …
